articles.title: Luigi Mangione’s lawyers seek to delay his state and federal trials
articles.description:
In a letter to US District Judge Margaret Garnett, Mangione's lawyers said that the current schedule — the state trial in June and the federal trial in September — would put him “in the position of needing to prepare for two complicated and serious trials at the same time.”

The post Luigi Mangione’s lawyers seek to delay his state and federal trials appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Luigi Mangione's lawyers request trial delays for state and federal cases.
State trial is scheduled for June; federal trial set for September 2026.
Delays could affect legal proceedings and community interest in the cases.

articles.title: The World Baseball Classic is thriving. Rob Manfred says MLB might consider moving it to midseason.
articles.description:
This year's WBC drew 1,619,839 fans for 47 games, a 24% increase over the previous high of 1,306,414 in 2023.

The post The World Baseball Classic is thriving. Rob Manfred says MLB might consider moving it to midseason. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: World Baseball Classic attracted 1,619,839 fans across 47 games this year.
Attendance increased by 24% compared to last year's 1,306,414 fans.
MLB may consider moving the WBC to midseason, impacting local baseball schedules.

articles.title: Cuba’s leader says US aggression would meet ‘impregnable resistance’
articles.description:
Díaz-Canel said on X late Tuesday that the Trump administration “publicly threatens” Cuba’s government almost daily with overthrowing it, and any act of aggression “will clash with an impregnable resistance.”

The post Cuba’s leader says US aggression would meet ‘impregnable resistance’ appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Cuba's leader Díaz-Canel warns of strong resistance to US aggression on X.
He claims the Trump administration threatens Cuba's government almost daily.
Boston residents may see increased tensions affecting local Cuban-American communities.

articles.title: César Chávez accused of sexually abusing labor rights leader Dolores Huerta
articles.description:

summary: Dolores Huerta accused César Chávez of sexual abuse after 60 years of silence.

Accusations may affect the legacy of labor rights in Boston's immigrant communities.

Local aviation safety concerns may rise following this tragic incident.

articles.title: Shrewsbury’s TJ Power finally found a home at Penn. Now he’s led the Quakers to the NCAA Tournament.
articles.description:
A few hours after delivering one of the most remarkable performances in Ivy League men’s basketball history, Penn junior TJ Power sat alone in his hotel room and let himself cry. “It’s emotional,” said Power, a Shrewsbury native. “A lot has gone into this three years in college. To have a moment like that, it […]

The post Shrewsbury’s TJ Power finally found a home at Penn. Now he’s led the Quakers to the NCAA Tournament. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Shrewsbury native TJ Power led Penn to the NCAA Tournament after a standout performance.
Power's emotional journey culminated in a historic game for the Quakers in March 2023.
His success highlights local talent from Boston suburbs in collegiate sports.

articles.title: WATCH: Bagpiper performs on St. Patrick’s Day in Boston
articles.description:
